file.content:
local st, msg = xpcall(foo, debug.traceback)
  assert(string.match(msg, "^[^ ]* @x123"))
  assert(string.find(msg, "in metamethod 'close'"))
end


do   -- errors due to non-closable values
  local function foo ()
    local x <close> = {}
    os.exit(false)    -- should not run
  end
  local stat, msg = pcall(foo)
  assert(not stat and
    string.find(msg, "variable 'x' got a non%-closable value"))

  local function foo ()
    local xyz <close> = setmetatable({}, {__close = print})
    getmetatable(xyz).__close = nil   -- remove metamethod
  end
  local stat, msg = pcall(foo)
  assert(not stat and string.find(msg, "metamethod 'close'"))

  local function foo ()
    local a1 <close> = func2close(function (_, msg)
      assert(string.find(msg, "number value"))
      error(12)
    end)
    local a2 <close> = setmetatable({}, {__close = print})
    local a3 <close> = func2close(function (_, msg)
      assert(msg == nil)
      error(123)
    end)
    getmetatable(a2).__close = 4   -- invalidate metamethod
  end
  local stat, msg = pcall(foo)
  assert(not stat and msg == 12)
end


do   -- tbc inside close methods
  local track = {}
  local function foo ()
    local x <close> = func2close(function ()
      local xx <close> = func2close(function (_, msg)
        assert(msg == nil)
        track[#track + 1] = "xx"
      end)
      track[#track + 1] = "x"
    end)
    track[#track + 1] = "foo"
    return 20, 30, 40
  end
  local a, b, c, d = foo()
  assert(a == 20 and b == 30 and c == 40 and d == nil)
  assert(track[1] == "foo" and track[2] == "x" and track[3] == "xx")

  -- again, with errors
  local track = {}
  local function foo ()

harness/run_official_test.sh

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
3	#
4	# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
5	# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
6	# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
7	# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
8	# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
9	# context.
10	#
11	# Usage:
12	#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua
13	
14	set -uo pipefail
15	
16	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
17	cd "$ROOT"
18	
19	TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
20	BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
21	OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
22	mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"
23	
24	if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
25	    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
26	    exit 2
27	fi
28	
29	TEST_FILE="$1"
30	if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
31	    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
32	    exit 2
33	fi
34	
35	if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
36	    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
37	    exit 2
38	fi
39	
40	BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
41	COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
42	OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"
43	
44	PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
45	_soft = true
46	_port = true
47	_nomsg = true
48	_U = false
49	arg = arg or {}
50	_G = _G or _ENV
51	if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
52	'
53	
54	{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"
55	
56	TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
57	export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"
58	
59	run_with_timeout() {
60	    local src_file="$1"
61	    local out_file="$2"
62	    local src
63	    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
64	    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
65	        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
66	    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
67	        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
68	    else
69	        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
70	        local pid=$!
71	        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
72	        local watcher=$!
73	        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
74	        local rc=$?
75	        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
76	        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
77	        return $rc
78	    fi
79	}
80	
81	run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true
82	
83	reached_exec=0
84	grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1
85	
86	if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
87	    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
88	    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
89	    exit 0
90	fi
91	
92	if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
93	    cause="stub"
94	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
95	    cause="parse-panic"
96	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
97	    cause="codegen-panic"
98	elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
99	    cause="runtime-panic"
100	elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
101	    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
102	        cause="runtime-error"
103	    else
104	        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
105	    fi
106	else
107	    cause="unknown"
108	fi
109	
110	printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
111	echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
112	{
113	    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
114	        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
115	} | head -n 5
116	echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
117	exit 1
118

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

content.content:
460	/// Finds the stack slot for vararg value number `n` (n is negative) in `ci`.
461	/// Returns `Some(pos)` and the name `b"(vararg)"` if found, else `None`.
462	///
463	/// C: `static const char *findvararg(CallInfo *ci, int n, StkId *pos)`
464	///
465	/// PORT NOTE: C sets `*pos` as an out-parameter. Rust returns an Option of the
466	/// stack index alongside the name.
467	fn find_vararg(ci: &CallInfo, n: i32) -> Option<(StackIdx, &'static [u8])> {
468	    // C: if (clLvalue(s2v(ci->func.p))->p->is_vararg)
469	    // TODO(port): accessing proto from ci requires LuaState; restructured to take
470	    // nextraargs and is_vararg directly. Phase B will wire this properly.
471	    if ci.is_vararg_func() {
472	        let nextra = ci.nextra_args();
473	        // C: if (n >= -nextra)  — 'n' is negative
474	        if n >= -(nextra as i32) {
475	            // C: *pos = ci->func.p - nextra - (n + 1);
476	            // PORT NOTE: pointer arithmetic converted to index arithmetic.
477	            // ci->func.p is the function slot; varargs are at func - nextra - 1 .. func - 1
478	            let pos = ci.func.wrapping_sub((nextra as i32 + n + 1) as u32);
479	            return Some((StackIdx(pos), b"(vararg)" as &[u8]));
480	        }
481	    }
482	    None
483	}
484

489	/// - Returns `None` if no such variable exists.
490	/// - If `pos` is `Some`, sets it to the variable's stack index.
491	///
492	/// C: `const char *luaG_findlocal(lua_State *L, CallInfo *ci, int n, StkId *pos)`
493	pub(crate) fn find_local<'a>(
494	    state: &'a LuaState,

delta.text: The

error message now correctly shows `'x'` instead of `'?'`. Build is green.

**Root cause:** `L
uaState::debug_find_local` in `crates/lua-vm/src/func.rs:785` was a Phase-B stub that always returned `None`, and `
find_local` in `crates/lua-vm/src/debug.rs:520` set `name = None` for the Lua-function branch instead of calling `
luaF_getlocalname`. As a result, `check_close_mth` always emitted `vari
able '?' got a non-closable value`, but the test on `locals.lua:504` expects the act

ual variable name (`'x'`).

**Fix:** Implemented `find_local` to call the existing `crate::func::get_local
_name(&proto, n, pc)` (returning `Option<Vec<u8>>` rather than `&'a
[u8]` since the proto borrow can't escape), wired `state.debug_find_local` to del
egate to it, and updated `check_close_mth` and the two `get_local`/`set_local` callers to consume the owned `Vec

<u8>`.

**New state:** Minimal repro now prints `variable 'x' got a non-closable value`, matching the test's expected pattern.
Did not re-run the full `locals.lua` (mega-loop will re-test) — the next assert at line 511 (the
`__close = nil` case) depends on a different code path (call-of-nil error path in `try_func
_tm`), which should now also benefit since `funcname_from_call` was already w
ired correctly.

content.text:
The error message now correctly shows `'x'` instead of `'?'`. Build is green.

**Root cause:** `LuaState::debug_find_local` in `crates/lua-vm/src/func.rs:785` was a Phase-B stub that always returned `None`, and `find_local` in `crates/lua-vm/src/debug.rs:520` set `name = None` for the Lua-function branch instead of calling `luaF_getlocalname`. As a result, `check_close_mth` always emitted `variable '?' got a non-closable value`, but the test on `locals.lua:504` expects the actual variable name (`'x'`).

**Fix:** Implemented `find_local` to call the existing `crate::func::get_local_name(&proto, n, pc)` (returning `Option<Vec<u8>>` rather than `&'a [u8]` since the proto borrow can't escape), wired `state.debug_find_local` to delegate to it, and updated `check_close_mth` and the two `get_local`/`set_local` callers to consume the owned `Vec<u8>`.

**New state:** Minimal repro now prints `variable 'x' got a non-closable value`, matching the test's expected pattern. Did not re-run the full `locals.lua` (mega-loop will re-test) — the next assert at line 511 (the `__close = nil` case) depends on a different code path (call-of-nil error path in `try_func_tm`), which should now also benefit since `funcname_from_call` was already wired correctly.

result:
The error message now correctly shows `'x'` instead of `'?'`. Build is green.

**Root cause:** `LuaState::debug_find_local` in `crates/lua-vm/src/func.rs:785` was a Phase-B stub that always returned `None`, and `find_local` in `crates/lua-vm/src/debug.rs:520` set `name = None` for the Lua-function branch instead of calling `luaF_getlocalname`. As a result, `check_close_mth` always emitted `variable '?' got a non-closable value`, but the test on `locals.lua:504` expects the actual variable name (`'x'`).

**Fix:** Implemented `find_local` to call the existing `crate::func::get_local_name(&proto, n, pc)` (returning `Option<Vec<u8>>` rather than `&'a [u8]` since the proto borrow can't escape), wired `state.debug_find_local` to delegate to it, and updated `check_close_mth` and the two `get_local`/`set_local` callers to consume the owned `Vec<u8>`.

**New state:** Minimal repro now prints `variable 'x' got a non-closable value`, matching the test's expected pattern. Did not re-run the full `locals.lua` (mega-loop will re-test) — the next assert at line 511 (the `__close = nil` case) depends on a different code path (call-of-nil error path in `try_func_tm`), which should now also benefit since `funcname_from_call` was already wired correctly.
